channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Paid Student Fellowship Opportunity with the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Students and young professionals seeking practical experience in international development, education policy, advocacy, and nonprofit communications now have an exciting opportunity through the Paid Student Fellowship offered by the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US). This fellowship provides students with hands-on experience working with a leading advocacy coalition that promotes universal access to quality and inclusive […]

The post Paid Student Fellowship Opportunity with the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students and young professionals seeking practical experience in international development, education policy, advocacy, and nonprofit communications now have an exciting opportunity through the Paid Student Fellowship offered by the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US). This fellowship provides students with hands-on experience working with a leading advocacy coalition that promotes universal access to quality and inclusive education worldwide.

The fellowship is designed to support students who are passionate about social change, global education rights, and policy advocacy. It allows participants to contribute meaningfully to international education campaigns while developing professional skills in communications, policy engagement, research, and nonprofit management.

The fellowship also offers financial compensation and flexible working hours suitable for students balancing academic commitments. Participants can gain valuable exposure to international education policy networks and build connections with organizations advocating for education as a fundamental human right.

About the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US)

The Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US) is a coalition of organizations working together to ensure that all children and youth worldwide have access to quality, inclusive, and equitable education. The organization focuses on advocacy efforts aimed at mobilizing political will in the United States and globally to support education initiatives.

The coalition collaborates with civil society organizations, policy leaders, educators, and international partners to promote education policies that support sustainable development and human rights. Education is recognized as a key driver of social, economic, and political progress, and GCE-US works to ensure it remains at the center of global development agendas.

Through this fellowship program, students gain a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of advocacy, policy development, communication, and coalition building.

Overview of the Paid Student Fellowship

The GCE-US Paid Student Fellowship provides students with the opportunity to participate in real-world advocacy efforts supporting global education initiatives.

Fellowship Key Details

  • Category: Fellowship / Internship

  • Compensation: Approximately $16.10 per hour

  • Work Schedule: 10–20 hours per week

  • Location: 100% remote (telework)

  • Preferred Locations: Washington, DC or New York City metro areas for occasional meetings

  • Duration: Flexible; preference for fellows who can continue for at least one academic year

  • Start Date: As soon as possible

  • Academic Credit: May be available depending on the student’s university

  • Application Method: Email submission of cover letter and resume

The fellowship is structured to allow students to work remotely while contributing to meaningful global education advocacy campaigns.

Responsibilities of the GCE-US Student Fellow

Fellows play an active role in supporting the coalition’s advocacy work, communication strategies, and member engagement. The fellowship provides a dynamic learning environment where participants gain practical experience in nonprofit operations and international policy advocacy.

Communications and Social Media Management

One of the key responsibilities of fellows is supporting GCE-US communication strategies. Fellows will:

  • Create and manage social media content across platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, and YouTube.

  • Design graphics and visual materials highlighting global education statistics and advocacy campaigns.

  • Monitor engagement and analyze social media campaign performance.

This experience helps fellows develop valuable digital communications skills applicable to nonprofit organizations, advocacy groups, and international development agencies.

Research and Policy Advocacy

Another important component of the fellowship involves conducting research on global education issues. Fellows will:

  • Research international education policies and global education trends.

  • Prepare presentations and reports for coalition members.

  • Support advocacy initiatives aimed at promoting education rights globally.

This research contributes to the organization’s efforts to influence policy decisions and strengthen global education systems.

Coalition Engagement and Partnership Development

GCE-US works with a wide network of partner organizations. Fellows help maintain these relationships through:

  • Communicating with member organizations

  • Coordinating coalition meetings

  • Engaging partners in collaborative advocacy campaigns

  • Supporting outreach to new organizations

This responsibility allows fellows to gain experience in stakeholder engagement and international collaboration.

Event Coordination and Meeting Support

Fellows assist in planning and coordinating meetings, events, and advocacy campaigns.

Typical responsibilities may include:

  • Preparing agendas for coalition meetings

  • Contacting speakers and participants

  • Scheduling and organizing meetings

  • Taking meeting minutes and tracking follow-up actions

  • Supporting fundraising and advocacy events

These tasks help fellows develop organizational and event management skills.

Content Creation and Publication

Fellows also contribute to producing educational and advocacy materials. Tasks may include:

  • Writing blog posts and articles

  • Coordinating the organization’s monthly newsletter

  • Updating website content

  • Publishing press releases and partner highlights

These responsibilities strengthen fellows’ writing, editing, and public communication abilities.

Qualifications and Eligibility Requirements

To ensure fellows can effectively contribute to the organization’s work, GCE-US has outlined several qualifications and expectations.

Academic Requirements

Applicants must be:

  • Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate degree program, or

  • Recently graduated from a recognized university.

Fields of study that align well with the fellowship include:

  • International development

  • Education policy

  • Public policy

  • Communications

  • Political science

  • Social sciences

  • Nonprofit management

Professional Skills and Competencies

Successful applicants should demonstrate the following skills:

  • Strong writing, editing, and communication skills

  • Ability to organize and manage multiple tasks

  • Experience or interest in advocacy and social impact initiatives

  • Knowledge of social media management and digital communications

  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively in remote environments

Applicants should also possess a strong attention to detail and excellent organizational skills.

Technical Skills

Preferred technical skills include familiarity with:

  • Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

  • Google Workspace tools

  • Zoom and remote collaboration platforms

  • Graphic design tools such as Canva

  • Website management platforms like WordPress

  • Data analytics tools such as Google Analytics

Although not mandatory, experience with these tools strengthens an applicant’s profile.

Language Requirements

The primary working language for the fellowship is English. However, additional language skills are considered an advantage.

Languages that may strengthen an application include:

  • Arabic

  • French

  • Portuguese

  • Spanish

  • International Sign Language

  • American Sign Language

Multilingual candidates are particularly valuable in international advocacy organizations working across different regions.

Who Can Apply for the GCE-US Fellowship?

Eligible Categories of Applicants

The opportunity is open to:

  • Undergraduate students

  • Graduate students

  • Recent university graduates

  • Students interested in international development and advocacy

Candidates from marginalized and underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.

Geographic Eligibility

Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States because the organization cannot sponsor work permits.

Therefore, eligible applicants typically include:

  • Students studying in the United States

  • U.S. citizens or permanent residents

  • International students with legal work authorization in the U.S.

Benefits of the GCE-US Paid Student Fellowship

Participating in this fellowship offers several professional and academic benefits.

Professional Development

Fellows gain real-world experience working with an international advocacy organization. This exposure helps participants understand how policy advocacy campaigns operate at national and global levels.

Networking Opportunities

The fellowship allows students to connect with:

  • Education policy experts

  • International development professionals

  • Nonprofit leaders

  • Advocacy organizations and coalition members

These connections can open doors to future employment opportunities.

Advocacy Experience

Fellows gain firsthand experience contributing to campaigns aimed at promoting education as a human right. This experience can be valuable for careers in:

  • International development

  • Public policy

  • Education reform

  • Human rights advocacy

Academic Integration

Because the fellowship offers flexible hours and potential academic credit, it is particularly suitable for students who want to combine practical experience with academic studies.

How to Apply for the GCE-US Paid Student Fellowship

Applicants must submit their application via email.

Required Application Documents

Applicants should send the following materials in a single document:

  1. Cover letter

  2. Resume

The subject line should include: “Fellowship Application.”

Apply here

Information to Include in the Cover Letter

Applicants must also address several questions in their cover letter:

  • Why they want to work in the role

  • How they learned about the opportunity

  • Their salary expectations

  • Availability and preferred work schedule

  • Academic program details

  • Confirmation of legal authorization to work in the United States

Applications should be sent to the email address listed on the official opportunity page.

Why This Fellowship Is Valuable for Future Global Leaders

Education advocacy remains one of the most important global development priorities. Access to quality education improves economic opportunity, promotes gender equality, and supports sustainable development worldwide.

The GCE-US fellowship allows students to participate directly in advocacy initiatives that influence international education policy and funding decisions. Through hands-on work with coalition members and policy advocates, fellows gain the experience necessary to become future leaders in education reform and global development.

This fellowship also equips students with practical skills in communication, research, nonprofit management, and coalition building, all of which are highly valued in the international development sector.

Application Deadline

22nd March 2026

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted).

The GCE-US Paid Student Fellowship is an excellent opportunity for students passionate about global education, advocacy, and nonprofit leadership. With paid compensation, flexible working hours, and the chance to contribute to international education campaigns, the fellowship provides a meaningful platform for students to build their careers while making a positive global impact.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Paid Student Fellowship Opportunity with the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Global Public Health Fellowship for Professionals Working in Epidemic Intelligence and Outbreak Response
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
JThe GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme 2026 is a prestigious international fellowship designed to strengthen global capacity in epidemic intelligence, outbreak response, and public health emergency preparedness. The program is jointly coordinated by the Robert Koch Institute (RKI), the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, and the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN). This […]

The post GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Global Public Health Fellowship for Professionals Working in Epidemic Intelligence and Outbreak Response appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
JThe GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme 2026 is a prestigious international fellowship designed to strengthen global capacity in epidemic intelligence, outbreak response, and public health emergency preparedness. The program is jointly coordinated by the Robert Koch Institute (RKI), the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, and the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN).

This six-month fellowship provides public health professionals with the opportunity to design and implement impactful projects that improve surveillance systems, strengthen outbreak response capabilities, and contribute to global health security. The program equips fellows with advanced skills in public health surveillance, emergency response, and epidemic intelligence while fostering collaboration with leading global health experts and institutions.

The fellowship is designed for professionals working within GOARN partner institutions and technical networks worldwide. Participants gain hands-on experience through mentorship, research, and collaborative projects while contributing to the development of innovative strategies that enhance global preparedness for future health emergencies.

Overview of the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme

The GOARN Berlin Fellowship is a six-month project-based training and research fellowship aimed at developing the next generation of global health leaders. Participants undertake a project related to epidemic intelligence, public health surveillance, outbreak control, or emergency preparedness.

The fellowship focuses on strengthening the capacity of institutions around the world to detect and respond to emerging public health threats. By working closely with experts from leading global health organizations, fellows gain practical insights and advanced knowledge that can be applied in their home institutions and countries.

Key objectives of the fellowship include:

  • Enhancing expertise in epidemic intelligence and disease surveillance

  • Strengthening global collaboration in outbreak response and public health preparedness

  • Supporting innovative research and operational projects that improve early warning systems

  • Developing leadership capacity among public health professionals

  • Building stronger international networks of public health experts

Through the fellowship, participants become part of a global network of professionals working together to improve health security worldwide.

Fellowship Structure and Tracks

Beginning with the 2026 cohort, the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme offers two flexible participation tracks, allowing fellows to tailor their experience to their professional circumstances and goals.

1. Berlin Track

Under the Berlin Track, fellows relocate to Berlin, Germany, where they are based at the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence and the Robert Koch Institute.

During their stay, fellows collaborate with global experts and participate in intensive training sessions, workshops, and project development activities. This track offers a fully immersive experience within one of the world’s leading public health ecosystems.

2. Country Track

The Country Track allows fellows to implement their projects while remaining in their home country or institution.

Although fellows work primarily within their local context, they still participate in joint training programs and collaborative activities in Berlin and Geneva. This flexible approach enables professionals who cannot relocate for the full six months to benefit from the fellowship while contributing directly to capacity development in their home institutions.

Both tracks provide fellows with equal opportunities to engage in mentorship, collaborative research, and global health networking.

Fellowship Duration

The GOARN Berlin Fellowship runs for six months, during which fellows focus on implementing their project while receiving expert mentorship and training.

The program is structured around:

  • Project development and implementation

  • Mentorship and technical guidance

  • Training workshops and seminars

  • Collaborative activities with global health experts

  • Cohort learning experiences

Throughout the fellowship, participants engage with professionals from international public health agencies, research institutions, and government health authorities.

Institutions Coordinating the Fellowship

The GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme is a collaborative initiative coordinated by several leading global health organizations:

Robert Koch Institute (RKI)

The Robert Koch Institute is Germany’s national public health institute and plays a central role in disease surveillance, epidemiological research, and health protection.

WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence

This World Health Organization hub focuses on improving global surveillance systems by using data and analytics to detect and respond to emerging health threats.

Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN)

GOARN is a global network of institutions and experts coordinated by the World Health Organization that responds to public health emergencies and disease outbreaks worldwide.

Together, these organizations provide fellows with access to cutting-edge research environments, advanced data tools, and global expertise in epidemic intelligence.

Fields and Areas of Work

Projects developed during the fellowship typically focus on areas such as:

  • Epidemic intelligence

  • Public health surveillance systems

  • Outbreak detection and response

  • Pandemic preparedness and response strategies

  • Risk assessment and emergency management

  • Early warning systems for infectious diseases

  • Data-driven public health decision making

These projects contribute directly to strengthening national and global capacities for disease surveillance and outbreak response.

Benefits of the GOARN Berlin Fellowship

Participants in the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme receive numerous professional and academic benefits, including:

Advanced Training

Fellows participate in training programs designed to strengthen skills in epidemic intelligence, outbreak response, and data analysis.

Mentorship from Global Experts

Participants receive mentorship from leading experts at the Robert Koch Institute, WHO Hub, and GOARN.

International Collaboration

The fellowship connects participants with global networks of public health professionals and institutions.

Practical Project Implementation

Fellows work on real-world projects that strengthen surveillance systems and outbreak preparedness in their home institutions.

Professional Networking

Participants build long-term collaborations with international researchers, policymakers, and public health experts.

Global Health Leadership Development

The program prepares fellows to become leaders in epidemic intelligence and public health emergency response.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme, candidates must meet several eligibility requirements.

Institutional Eligibility

Applicants must be working at a GOARN partner institution or technical network. Only professionals affiliated with these institutions are eligible to apply.

Professional Background

Candidates should be public health professionals with experience in areas such as:

  • Epidemiology

  • Disease surveillance

  • Emergency preparedness and response

  • Public health research

  • Risk communication and outbreak management

Project Proposal

Applicants are typically required to submit a project proposal related to epidemic intelligence, surveillance, or outbreak response.

Professional Commitment

Candidates should demonstrate a strong commitment to improving public health systems and contributing to global health security.

Eligible Countries and Regions

The fellowship is open to applicants from institutions that are members of the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network.

GOARN partner institutions are located across multiple regions, including:

  • Africa

  • Asia

  • Europe

  • The Americas

  • The Middle East

  • Oceania

Because GOARN is a global network, professionals from many countries worldwide may be eligible to apply if their organization is part of the network.

Who Should Apply

The fellowship is ideal for professionals who:

  • Work in national public health institutes

  • Serve in ministries of health

  • Conduct public health research

  • Participate in outbreak response teams

  • Work in international health organizations

  • Contribute to epidemic surveillance systems

The program is particularly beneficial for professionals seeking to strengthen their expertise in epidemic intelligence and contribute to the improvement of disease surveillance systems in their home countries.

Impact of the Fellowship

The GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme has already supported public health professionals from multiple countries in developing innovative projects that strengthen surveillance systems and outbreak preparedness.

Fellows from previous cohorts have worked on projects such as:

  • Improving early warning systems for infectious disease outbreaks

  • Strengthening national surveillance platforms

  • Enhancing risk assessment frameworks for public health emergencies

  • Developing new approaches to epidemic intelligence

These initiatives contribute to building stronger, data-driven public health systems worldwide.

How to Apply

Applicants interested in the fellowship should follow these steps:

  1. Confirm that their institution is a GOARN partner organization.

  2. Contact their institution’s GOARN focal point for application guidance.

  3. Prepare a project proposal related to epidemic intelligence or outbreak response.

  4. Complete the official application process through the program’s designated application channels.

Because the program accepts a limited number of fellows, applicants are encouraged to submit strong proposals that demonstrate innovation, feasibility, and potential impact on global health.

Apply here

Application Deadline

16th March 2026 at 17:00 CET

Applicants should ensure all required documents and project proposals are submitted before the deadline.The GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme is one of the most impactful global health training opportunities available to public health professionals today. By combining advanced training, real-world project implementation, and collaboration with leading global health institutions, the fellowship equips participants with the knowledge and skills necessary to strengthen outbreak preparedness and response worldwide. Professionals working in public health institutions that are part of the GOARN network are strongly encouraged to apply for this prestigious fellowship and contribute to building stronger global health systems capable of responding to future epidemics and pandemics.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Global Public Health Fellowship for Professionals Working in Epidemic Intelligence and Outbreak Response appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: McKinsey Forward Program 2026: Fully Free Global Career Accelerator for Young Professionals
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
If you are a young professional looking to future-proof your career, build high-income skills, and gain access to a global network—then the McKinsey Forward Program 2026 is one of the most powerful free opportunities available right now. Offered by McKinsey & Company, this program is designed to equip early-career professionals with essential workplace, digital, and leadership […]

The post McKinsey Forward Program 2026: Fully Free Global Career Accelerator for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a young professional looking to future-proof your career, build high-income skills, and gain access to a global network—then the McKinsey Forward Program 2026 is one of the most powerful free opportunities available right now.

Offered by McKinsey & Company, this program is designed to equip early-career professionals with essential workplace, digital, and leadership skills required to succeed in today’s rapidly evolving job market.

What Is the McKinsey Forward Program?

The McKinsey Forward Program is a free, structured online learning experience that helps young professionals develop practical, real-world skills that are immediately applicable in the workplace.

The program has already impacted over 500,000 learners globally, helping them build confidence, resilience, and career-ready competencies.

It focuses on bridging the gap between academic knowledge and workplace performance—making it ideal for early-career individuals trying to stand out in competitive job markets.

Why the McKinsey Forward Program Is a Game-Changer

Unlike many online courses, this program is not just theoretical. It is designed to deliver practical transformation in how you think, communicate, and solve problems.

Key Benefits

  • Gain high-demand professional skills used in global organizations

  • Learn structured problem-solving techniques used by top consultants

  • Build communication and leadership confidence

  • Develop digital and AI awareness skills

  • Earn a globally recognized McKinsey digital badge

  • Join a global alumni network of professionals

Participants also gain continued access to networking opportunities, learning resources, and career development support after completing the program.

Skills You Will Learn

During the 10-week program, participants develop core workplace competencies that employers value worldwide.

Core Skills Covered

  • Structured problem-solving and critical thinking

  • Effective communication and storytelling

  • Adaptability and resilience in changing environments

  • Emotional intelligence and teamwork

  • Relationship building and collaboration

  • Digital and AI fundamentals for modern workplaces

These skills help participants turn business challenges into opportunities, communicate persuasively, and navigate complex work environments effectively.

Who Should Apply? (Target Audience)

This opportunity is specifically designed for:

  • Young professionals in early stages of their careers

  • Recent graduates or individuals transitioning into the workforce

  • Freelancers, entrepreneurs, and job seekers

  • Individuals looking to upgrade workplace and digital skills

  • Professionals with less than 5 years of experience

It is particularly beneficial for those who want to:

  • Improve employability

  • Transition into better roles

  • Gain international exposure

  • Build a strong professional network

Eligibility Criteria (Who Qualifies)

To apply for the McKinsey Forward Program 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Basic Requirements

  • Must be currently residing in any African country

  • Must have less than 5 years of total work experience

  • Must have completed at least high school education

  • Must have basic proficiency in English (program is conducted in English)

Work Experience Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be employed, self-employed, OR

  • Have prior experience (including internships/apprenticeships), OR

  • Be planning to start working within the next 12 months

Special Consideration

Even if you have more than 5 years of experience, you may still qualify if:

  • You work in NGOs or nonprofit sectors

  • You are in a non-managerial role

  • You are in a non-corporate setting

Eligible Countries and Regions

  • All African countries are eligible

  • The program is fully virtual, so there are no travel restrictions

This makes it highly accessible for applicants from:

  • Kenya

  • Nigeria

  • Ghana

  • South Africa

  • Egypt

  • And all other African nations

Program Structure and Learning Experience

The program is divided into structured learning phases:

1. Core Skills Level (10 Weeks)

  • Approximately 2 hours per week

  • Interactive lessons and real-world exercises

  • Practical application of concepts in your daily work

2. Network Level (Post-Completion)

  • Access to global alumni community

  • Invitations to networking events

  • Continued learning and leadership opportunities

This blended approach ensures both skill acquisition and long-term career growth.

Certification and Career Value

Upon successful completion, participants receive:

  • A McKinsey.org Forward Digital Badge

  • Recognition that can be added to LinkedIn and resumes

  • Access to a global professional network

This certification demonstrates your ability to:

  • Think critically

  • Solve problems effectively

  • Communicate with impact

  • Adapt to modern workplace challenges

How to Apply

To apply for the McKinsey Forward Program:

  • Visit the official McKinsey Forward webpage

  • Complete the online application form

  • Submit your details and wait for confirmation

Apply here

Why You Should Not Miss This Opportunity

In today’s competitive job market, having technical knowledge alone is no longer enough. Employers are increasingly looking for candidates who can:

  • Think strategically

  • Communicate effectively

  • Adapt quickly

  • Work collaboratively

The McKinsey Forward Program equips you with exactly these skills—completely free of charge. For young professionals across Africa, this is not just a training program—it is a career transformation opportunity.

Deadline

20th April 2026

The McKinsey Forward Program 2026 stands out as one of the most accessible and impactful global learning opportunities for early-career professionals.With zero cost, flexible learning, and world-class content, it offers a rare chance to gain skills that are typically taught in elite corporate environments.

For more information visit here
For more global opportunities click here

The post McKinsey Forward Program 2026: Fully Free Global Career Accelerator for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Women Leadership in Climate Action Free Online Course 2026 (With Certificate) – Fully Funded Global Opportunity to Build Climate Leadership Skills
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The “Developing Skills for Women Leadership in Climate Action” course is a globally recognized online training program offered through UN CC:Learn. This course is designed to empower women and girls across the world with the knowledge, tools, and leadership skills necessary to actively contribute to solving the global climate crisis. In a time when climate […]

The post UN Women Leadership in Climate Action Free Online Course 2026 (With Certificate) – Fully Funded Global Opportunity to Build Climate Leadership Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The “Developing Skills for Women Leadership in Climate Action” course is a globally recognized online training program offered through UN CC:Learn. This course is designed to empower women and girls across the world with the knowledge, tools, and leadership skills necessary to actively contribute to solving the global climate crisis.

In a time when climate change is one of the most urgent global challenges, this opportunity provides a structured and accessible pathway for individuals—especially women—to gain the competencies required to lead impactful climate initiatives in their communities and beyond.

This course is especially relevant for individuals from developing countries, Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and Oceania who are looking to build careers or projects in sustainability, climate action, and environmental leadership.

About the UN CC:Learn Platform

UN CC:Learn is a collaborative initiative involving more than 30 multilateral organizations that aims to strengthen global capacity to tackle climate change through education and training. It was launched to support countries and individuals in developing practical knowledge and skills to mitigate and adapt to climate change effectively.

The platform offers a wide range of free online courses covering climate science, green economy, sustainable development, and environmental policy, making it one of the most trusted global learning hubs in this field.

Overview of the Women Leadership in Climate Action Course

The Developing Skills for Women Leadership in Climate Action course is a self-paced e-learning program that focuses on empowering women and girls to take leadership roles in climate action initiatives.

Key Course Details:

  • Course Type: Self-paced online training

  • Duration: Approximately 9 hours (+ 3 hours of practical exercises)

  • Languages Available: English, French, Spanish

  • Certification: Available (USD 30 optional fee)

  • Access: Free learning content

  • Mode: 100% online

The course combines theoretical knowledge with practical tools, ensuring that participants not only understand climate change but can also implement real-world solutions.

Why You Should Apply for This Course

This course is more than just a learning experience—it is a transformational opportunity to become part of the global climate solution.

Key Benefits:

  1. Gain In-Demand Climate Knowledge

    • Understand climate science, sustainability, and environmental challenges.

    • Learn how climate change affects different populations, especially women.

  2. Develop Leadership Skills

    • Build confidence to lead climate initiatives.

    • Learn how to influence stakeholders and mobilize communities.

  3. Practical Project Development

    • Gain tools to design and implement climate action projects.

    • Strengthen existing environmental initiatives.

  4. Global Recognition

    • Earn a certificate from a United Nations-affiliated platform.

  5. Flexible Learning

    • Study at your own pace from anywhere in the world.

Course Structure and Modules

The course is divided into four comprehensive modules, each designed to build your expertise step by step:

Module 1 & 2: Climate Change Fundamentals

  • Introduction to climate science

  • Causes and impacts of climate change

  • Climate mitigation and adaptation strategies

  • Social inequalities and climate justice

Module 3 & 4: Leadership and Action

  • Designing inclusive climate projects

  • Leadership and communication skills

  • Influencing decision-makers

  • Building support for climate initiatives

Each module takes approximately 2.5 hours, making the course manageable even for busy professionals.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

This opportunity is open to a wide range of participants globally.

Eligible Categories:

  • Women and girls interested in climate action

  • Students (undergraduate and postgraduate)

  • Young professionals and early-career individuals

  • NGO workers and community leaders

  • Environmental activists

  • Policy enthusiasts and researchers

Academic Requirements:

  • No formal degree required

  • Basic understanding of environmental issues is helpful but not mandatory

Geographic Eligibility:

  • Open to ALL countries worldwide

  • Strongly encouraged for participants from:

    • Africa

    • Asia

    • Latin America

    • Developing and emerging economies

Who Should Take This Course?

This course is ideal for individuals who:

  • Want to start a career in climate change or sustainability

  • Are already working in environmental or social sectors

  • Have ideas for climate-related projects but lack direction

  • Want to influence climate policy or community initiatives

  • Are passionate about gender equality and environmental justice

Skills You Will Gain

By the end of the course, participants will be able to:

  • Explain key climate change concepts

  • Analyze the impact of climate change on women

  • Identify opportunities for climate action

  • Design and implement inclusive climate projects

  • Communicate and advocate for environmental change effectively

These skills are highly valuable in careers related to sustainability, development, policy, and environmental science.

Certification Details

While the course content is completely free, participants can opt to receive a digital certificate upon completion by paying a small fee (approximately USD 30).

This certificate can be used to:

  • Enhance your CV or resume

  • Strengthen job and scholarship applications

  • Demonstrate commitment to climate action

Why This Opportunity Matters Globally

Climate change is a global crisis requiring inclusive and diverse leadership. Women, especially in vulnerable regions, are disproportionately affected by climate impacts but are also powerful agents of change.

This course bridges the gap by:

  • Promoting gender-inclusive climate leadership

  • Providing accessible education to underserved populations

  • Empowering grassroots climate action

According to global climate learning initiatives, building awareness and skills is critical for enabling individuals and communities to effectively respond to climate challenges.

How to Apply

To enroll in the course:

  1. Visit the official UN CC:Learn platform

  2. Create a free account

  3. Enroll in the course

  4. Start learning immediately

Since the course is self-paced, there is no strict deadline, and you can begin at any time of the year.

Apply here

The UN Women Leadership in Climate Action Course 2026 is a powerful opportunity for anyone looking to build a meaningful career or initiative in climate change and sustainability. With no application restrictions, global accessibility, and high-quality UN-backed content, this course stands out as one of the best free online climate leadership programs available today.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post UN Women Leadership in Climate Action Free Online Course 2026 (With Certificate) – Fully Funded Global Opportunity to Build Climate Leadership Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship at Oxford University for African Conservation Professionals
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship Programme is a prestigious conservation fellowship designed specifically for African professionals working to protect biodiversity. Hosted at the University of Oxford, the programme provides conservation practitioners, policymakers, and researchers with an opportunity to collaborate with leading experts while accessing world-class academic resources. Launched in September 2021 and supported […]

The post Fully Funded A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship at Oxford University for African Conservation Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship Programme is a prestigious conservation fellowship designed specifically for African professionals working to protect biodiversity. Hosted at the University of Oxford, the programme provides conservation practitioners, policymakers, and researchers with an opportunity to collaborate with leading experts while accessing world-class academic resources.

Launched in September 2021 and supported by the A. G. Leventis Foundation, the fellowship is run by E. J. Milner-Gulland, the Tasso Leventis Professor of Biodiversity at the Department of Biology. The initiative is coordinated through the Interdisciplinary Centre for Conservation Science (ICCS), which focuses on bridging academic research with practical conservation work.

The fellowship aims to strengthen biodiversity conservation across Africa by enabling professionals to gain advanced training, build global networks, and collaborate with researchers at Oxford.

About the Fellowship Programme

The fellowship offers African conservation professionals the chance to spend up to three months in Oxford, United Kingdom, working closely with researchers and academics. Fellows can join any department within the University as long as their project aligns with biodiversity conservation.

During their stay, fellows benefit from:

  • Specialized training courses and workshops

  • Opportunities to develop research papers and conservation strategies

  • Access to Oxford’s research facilities and academic resources

  • Networking with global conservation experts

  • Collaboration with leading researchers in biodiversity science

In addition to the in-person residency at Oxford, the programme also provides remote mentoring, training, and networking opportunities both before and after the fellowship visit.

Each year, three fellows are selected for the programme, making it a highly competitive opportunity.

Core Objectives of the Fellowship

The programme is built around three key goals designed to enhance conservation impact in Africa:

1. Strengthening Conservation Leadership

The fellowship supports professionals from NGOs, government institutions, and private sector organizations who are involved in designing and implementing biodiversity conservation initiatives. Participants gain new skills, strengthen their professional networks, and improve their capacity to deliver impactful conservation outcomes in their home countries.

2. Bridging Research and Real-World Conservation

Another major objective is to ensure that academic research at Oxford reflects real conservation challenges. By collaborating with practitioners from Africa, the university ensures its research is practical, relevant, and applicable on the ground.

3. Building Long-Term Partnerships

The programme fosters long-term collaboration between Oxford researchers and conservation practitioners. These partnerships strengthen knowledge exchange and support sustainable conservation strategies across Africa.

Fellowship Activities and Opportunities

The fellowship provides a supportive environment where participants can focus on developing their conservation work. Fellows are encouraged to use their time at Oxford to:

  • Analyse and maximise the value of their conservation datasets

  • Develop new biodiversity conservation strategies

  • Design and test innovative approaches to conservation challenges

  • Attend professional training courses

  • Build partnerships with international collaborators

  • Share knowledge and experiences with Oxford researchers and students

These activities contribute to more effective biodiversity conservation efforts across Africa.

Cultural and Professional Experience in the UK

Beyond academic engagement, the fellowship offers a rich cultural experience in the United Kingdom.

Participants have opportunities to:

  • Visit Oxford’s historic colleges and world-renowned museums

  • Attend traditional college banquets

  • Experience cultural events such as Evensong

  • Participate in activities like punting on the River Cherwell

  • Explore birdwatching and other nature activities

These experiences allow fellows to immerse themselves in British culture while forming meaningful friendships and professional relationships.

Mentorship and Support System

To ensure fellows receive strong support during their stay, each participant is paired with:

  • An Oxford academic mentor who works in their area of expertise

  • An early-career researcher “buddy” who provides academic guidance and social support

This mentoring structure helps fellows navigate both academic work and daily life in Oxford while fostering collaborative learning.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship Programme plays a vital role in strengthening conservation leadership across Africa. By connecting practitioners with academic experts and global networks, the programme helps transform research insights into practical conservation solutions.

Ultimately, the fellowship aims to support more effective and sustainable biodiversity conservation across Africa, ensuring long-term protection of ecosystems and wildlife.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is open to a wide range of professionals involved in biodiversity conservation.

Senior Professionals

Senior staff from NGOs, government agencies, and businesses may benefit from stepping away from their daily responsibilities to:

  • Develop new conservation strategies

  • Strengthen partnerships

  • Write policy or research outputs

  • Build international collaborations

Early-Career Professionals

Early-career conservation professionals and researchers are also encouraged to apply, especially those who wish to:

  • Learn new research and analytical skills

  • Analyse and publish their conservation datasets

  • Expand their international professional networks

Eligibility Criteria

The fellowship has a clear eligibility requirement:

  • Applicants must be African nationals.

Candidates should be actively working on biodiversity conservation in sectors such as NGOs, government institutions, academia, or businesses involved in environmental sustainability.

Deadline is 31st March 2026.

Click Here, to apply.

Discover more similar opportunities by clicking  here. 

The post Fully Funded A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship at Oxford University for African Conservation Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Young Leaders to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young African leaders who are driving impactful initiatives that support vulnerable and marginalized youth in transitioning from education to sustainable employment or entrepreneurship. This prestigious scholarship will support 25 young changemakers from across Africa to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape […]

The post Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Young Leaders to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young African leaders who are driving impactful initiatives that support vulnerable and marginalized youth in transitioning from education to sustainable employment or entrepreneurship. This prestigious scholarship will support 25 young changemakers from across Africa to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa, scheduled to take place from 3–6 November 2026.

The scholarship is offered through a partnership between the Z Zurich Foundation and One Young World, an international organization that convenes one of the most influential global communities of young leaders working to address the world’s most pressing social, economic, and environmental challenges.

The Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship specifically focuses on empowering African youth leaders whose initiatives promote social equity through education, employability, entrepreneurship, and inclusive economic opportunities. Selected scholars will gain access to leadership training, international networking opportunities, and a global platform to amplify their work.

Applications for the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026 open on 16 March 2026 and close on 4 May 2026.

About the Z Zurich Foundation

The Z Zurich Foundation is committed to building brighter futures for vulnerable populations worldwide. The foundation prioritizes preventive action in addressing some of society’s most urgent and complex challenges.

Its work focuses on four major pillars:

  1. Adapting to climate change

  2. Improving mental well-being

  3. Enabling social equity

  4. Responding to crises

These areas address systemic barriers that affect individuals and communities across the globe. By supporting emerging leaders, the foundation aims to empower individuals who can drive sustainable solutions within their communities.

The Z Zurich Foundation believes that creating lasting social impact requires connection, collaboration, and grassroots leadership. This philosophy underpins its partnership with the One Young World Summit.

About the One Young World Summit

The One Young World Summit is one of the world’s most prominent global gatherings for young leaders. Each year, the summit brings together thousands of young changemakers, entrepreneurs, activists, and innovators from across the globe.

Participants attend leadership sessions, networking events, and panel discussions led by influential global leaders from sectors such as:

  • Government

  • Business

  • Non-profit organizations

  • Academia

  • Social entrepreneurship

The summit provides a platform for young leaders to exchange ideas, develop innovative solutions, and collaborate on initiatives addressing global challenges.

Through scholarships such as the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship, One Young World ensures that emerging leaders from underserved regions have access to this transformative experience.

Why the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship Focuses on Africa

Africa is the youngest continent in the world, with over 60 percent of its population under the age of 25. By 2030, African youth are expected to represent one-fifth of the global youth population.

Despite this demographic advantage, many African countries face significant challenges, including:

  • Limited employment opportunities for young people

  • Fragmented education and skills development systems

  • Unequal access to finance and digital technologies

  • Climate change impacts

  • Economic instability and conflict

  • Structural inequalities affecting vulnerable populations

These challenges create barriers that prevent young people from transitioning successfully from learning to earning.

Supporting youth-led grassroots initiatives is therefore essential. Young leaders who understand their communities are uniquely positioned to develop innovative solutions that address local challenges and empower others.

The Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship aims to identify and empower these leaders, enabling them to amplify their impact and influence systems that shape economic and social opportunities across Africa.

Overview of the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026

The scholarship will select 25 outstanding young leaders from Africa who are working on initiatives that support youth in overcoming barriers to education, employment, or entrepreneurship.

Successful applicants will attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa, and will also participate in a series of leadership development programs designed to enhance their capacity as changemakers.

These activities include:

  • A pre-summit leadership preparation program

  • Networking sessions with peers and experts

  • A pre-summit connection day in Cape Town

  • Post-summit engagement and reflection activities

  • Potential eligibility for an additional leadership fellowship

The goal of the program is to help participants expand their leadership skills, strengthen their networks, and scale their social impact initiatives.

Scholarship Benefits

Selected Z Zurich Foundation Scholars will receive a fully funded package that covers participation in the One Young World Summit as well as additional leadership development opportunities.

1. Full Participation in the One Young World Summit

Scholars will participate in the One Young World Summit 2026, held in Cape Town from 3–6 November 2026.

This global event will allow scholars to engage with other young leaders, industry experts, and international decision-makers.

2. Accommodation

Scholars will receive hotel accommodation in Cape Town from 1–6 November 2026.

3. Round-Trip Airfare

The scholarship includes return international flights to and from Cape Town, South Africa.

4. Meals and Catering

The scholarship provides full catering throughout the summit, including breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

5. Local Transportation

Transportation between summit venues in Cape Town will be covered.

Additional Leadership Development Opportunities

Beyond summit participation, scholars will engage in curated leadership development activities designed to deepen their impact.

1. Pre-Summit Engagement and Preparation Program

Between September and October 2026, scholars will participate in a virtual leadership development program that focuses on:

  • Personal leadership development

  • Preparing for effective participation in the summit

  • Learning from expert contributors

  • Developing strategies for scaling social impact

2. Pre-Summit Connection Day

On 2 November 2026, scholars will participate in an in-person event in Cape Town that allows participants to:

  • Connect with fellow scholars

  • Align on shared goals

  • Engage with the Z Zurich Foundation

3. Post-Summit Debrief Session

A post-summit online session will take place in late November 2026, allowing scholars to reflect on their experiences and plan future impact strategies.

4. Opportunity for Post-Summit Fellowship

Scholars will also have the opportunity to apply for a one-year Post-Summit Fellowship through the Z Zurich Foundation Global Changemakers Program.

This fellowship provides additional support and capacity-building resources to help leaders scale their initiatives.

Only a limited number of scholars will be selected for this additional opportunity.

Focus Areas for the Scholarship

The Z Zurich Foundation is seeking leaders who are implementing initiatives that help vulnerable youth transition successfully from education to employment or entrepreneurship.

Priority focus areas include the following.

1. Youth Employability

Initiatives that support young people in accessing meaningful employment opportunities.

Examples include:

  • Aligning education and vocational training with labor market demands

  • Improving pathways from education to employment

  • Addressing barriers that prevent young people from completing education

2. Youth Entrepreneurship

Programs that enable youth to create sustainable livelihoods through entrepreneurship.

These initiatives may focus on:

  • Financial literacy

  • Access to funding

  • Business development support

  • Income generation strategies

3. Digital Inclusion and Future of Work

Projects that help bridge the digital divide by:

  • Expanding access to digital technologies

  • Building digital and future-ready skills

  • Connecting youth with emerging employment opportunities

Learn more about our work: Social equity | Z Zurich Foundation.

Cross-Cutting Priorities

Across all focus areas, the Z Zurich Foundation is particularly interested in initiatives that:

  • Promote safe, decent, and productive employment

  • Support displaced or refugee youth

  • Empower marginalized communities

  • Improve youth mental well-being

  • Strengthen climate-resilient and green livelihoods

  • Create opportunities in rural or agricultural sectors

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following criteria to qualify for the scholarship.

  1. Age between 18 and 35 years by the date of the summit in November 2026.

  2. Must be nationals and residents of African countries.

  3. Demonstrate measurable impact aligned with the Enabling Social Equity pillar of the Z Zurich Foundation.

  4. Be leading an early-stage initiative that does not yet have major corporate or institutional funding.

  5. Lead a formally registered organization with a not-for-profit mission.

  6. The organization must have a bank account registered in its name.

  7. Serve in a primary leadership role within the initiative.

  8. Commit to participating in the Pre-Summit Engagement and Preparation Program.

  9. Participate in the Post-Summit Debrief Session.

  10. Be willing to actively collaborate and engage with fellow scholars before, during, and after the summit.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026 is 4 May 2026.

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early.

How to Apply

Applicants must submit their application through the Official One Young World Application Platform.

Applications will be reviewed based on:

  • Leadership potential

  • Social impact of the applicant’s initiative

  • Alignment with the foundation’s social equity goals

  • Commitment to community development

Click HERE to learn more about the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026.

Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Young Leaders to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NYU Stern Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists in New York
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship 2026 at NYU Stern School of Business, a unique opportunity for journalists worldwide to deepen their understanding of the economic forces shaping climate change. The fellowship brings selected participants to New York University’s Stern School of Business in Greenwich Village, New York City, for a […]

The post NYU Stern Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists in New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship 2026 at NYU Stern School of Business, a unique opportunity for journalists worldwide to deepen their understanding of the economic forces shaping climate change. The fellowship brings selected participants to New York University’s Stern School of Business in Greenwich Village, New York City, for a two-day immersive program focused on the intersection of climate change, financial markets, and the global economy.

Climate change is rapidly transforming industries, policies, and financial systems. For journalists reporting on environmental issues, understanding climate economics is increasingly essential. This prestigious fellowship is designed to help journalists interpret complex economic developments and report more effectively on climate-related topics.

Program Overview

The Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship will take place September 17–18, 2026, at NYU Stern’s campus in New York City. The program brings together a small cohort of journalists who will learn from globally recognized experts in climate economics, finance, and policy.

Participants will attend lectures, discussions, and networking events designed to strengthen their reporting skills and deepen their knowledge of the economic impacts of climate change.

The fellowship is organized by the Climate Finance Initiative at NYU Stern and includes contributions from faculty across New York University.

Financial Support

NYU Stern fully funds the fellowship program.

Selected fellows will receive:

  • $2,000 stipend for journalists residing outside the New York Metro Area to cover travel and accommodation

  • $1,000 stipend for journalists residing within the New York Metro Area

  • All program costs covered, including meals and event participation

This support ensures that journalists from different regions can participate without financial barriers.

What Fellows Will Learn

The program is designed for journalists who want to better understand how climate change influences economic systems and financial markets.

Sessions will explore key topics such as:

  • Climate risks to financial stability

  • The impact of climate change on housing and insurance markets

  • Corporate decision-making in response to climate risks

  • Climate regulation and carbon markets

  • Biodiversity loss as an economic risk

  • Renewable energy and electric vehicle market economics

  • Emerging climate finance regulations and legislation

Participants will also explore pressing questions such as the economics behind carbon pricing, carbon border adjustments, cap-and-trade systems, and the social cost of carbon.

In addition to academic sessions, fellows will engage in networking and social events that provide opportunities to connect with other journalists and NYU faculty.

Who Should Apply

The fellowship welcomes applications from:

  • Reporters

  • Editors

  • Freelance journalists

Applicants should have an interest in covering the intersection of climate change and the global economy. Prior training in economics is not required, making the program accessible to journalists from diverse reporting backgrounds.

Although some discussions may reference U.S. policies and institutions, journalists based outside the United States are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following materials:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)

  • Cover letter explaining motivation for applying

  • Samples of published work

Fellowship recipients will be selected based on the quality of these materials, particularly the applicant’s demonstrated interest in climate and economic reporting.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: April 20, 2026

  • Notification of Selected Fellows: Late May 2026

  • Program Dates: September 17–18, 2026

  • Location: NYU Stern School of Business, Greenwich Village, New York City

The fellowship takes place shortly before Climate Week NYC, offering participants additional opportunities to cover major climate-related events and discussions.

Why This Fellowship Matters

As climate change reshapes financial systems, markets, and public policy, journalists play a critical role in translating complex economic developments for global audiences.

The NYU Stern Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship equips journalists with the knowledge, tools, and expert connections needed to produce insightful and impactful climate reporting.

By participating in this program, journalists gain a deeper understanding of climate economics while building a global network of professionals committed to covering one of the most important stories of our time.

Interested journalists are encouraged to apply before the deadline and take advantage of this rare opportunity to learn from leading experts in climate economics.

The post NYU Stern Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists in New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Transformative Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 for Future Global Health Leaders
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Prestigious Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for aspiring public health leaders to pursue a Master of Public Health (MPH) degree with full tuition support at Cornell University. This fellowship program is designed to train the next generation of professionals who will tackle some of the world’s most pressing public health […]

The post Transformative Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 for Future Global Health Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Prestigious Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for aspiring public health leaders to pursue a Master of Public Health (MPH) degree with full tuition support at Cornell University. This fellowship program is designed to train the next generation of professionals who will tackle some of the world’s most pressing public health challenges.

In today’s rapidly changing world, societies face complex health threats that require innovative solutions and skilled leadership. Climate change, growing health inequities, emerging infectious diseases, and the erosion of trust in science are just a few of the challenges affecting global health systems. The Cornell Public Health Fellowship was established to prepare talented students to address these interconnected issues and rebuild resilient public health systems.

Through this fellowship, selected participants will receive full tuition support to pursue Cornell’s two-year Master of Public Health program or the 12-month accelerated MPH program for candidates who meet the eligibility requirements.

The application deadline for the Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 is March 23, 2026.

Cornell Fellowship Program Overview

The Cornell Public Health Fellowship is designed to equip future public health professionals with the knowledge, leadership skills, and practical experience needed to solve complex global health challenges.

Fellows will study within Cornell’s interdisciplinary academic environment, where students collaborate with faculty and experts from multiple disciplines. The program combines academic training with applied learning experiences that allow students to translate theoretical knowledge into practical solutions.

Students enrolled in the program will gain exposure to community-based learning opportunities, policy discussions, and applied research projects that address real-world health challenges. This experiential approach prepares fellows to work effectively across public health systems, government agencies, international organizations, and non-profit sectors.

Academic Training in the Cornell Fellowship

The Cornell MPH program emphasizes interdisciplinary learning and community engagement. Fellows will work closely with faculty members who specialize in diverse fields of public health and global health policy.

The curriculum focuses on building strong competencies in public health research, policy analysis, epidemiology, environmental health, and health systems leadership. Students will also gain practical experience through fieldwork, collaborative projects, and applied research opportunities.

This comprehensive academic approach prepares fellows to address emerging health challenges while contributing to evidence-based public health policies and programs.

Cornell Fellowship Concentration Areas

Students selected for the Cornell Public Health Fellowship will choose one of four specialized concentration areas within the MPH program.

Food Systems and Health

This concentration examines the relationship between food systems, nutrition, and public health. Students explore how food production, distribution, and access influence health outcomes and societal well-being.

Environment, Climate and Health

This specialization focuses on the intersection between environmental changes and public health. Students study the health impacts of climate change, environmental degradation, and pollution.

Infectious Disease Epidemiology

Students in this concentration learn how to monitor, analyze, and respond to infectious disease outbreaks. Training focuses on epidemiological methods used to track and control diseases at local, national, and global levels.

Emergency Preparedness and Management

This concentration prepares students to respond effectively to public health emergencies such as pandemics, natural disasters, and humanitarian crises.

Benefits of the Cornell Public Health Fellowship

The Cornell Public Health Fellowship provides several important benefits to selected students.

These include:

• Full tuition support for the Master of Public Health degree

• Access to world-class faculty and interdisciplinary research

• Opportunities for community-engaged learning

• Hands-on experience translating theory into practice

• Specialized training in key public health concentration areas

The fellowship allows students to focus on their academic and professional development without the burden of tuition costs.

Career Opportunities After the Fellowship

Graduates of the Cornell MPH program pursue careers across multiple sectors of public health.

Potential career paths include:

• Government health agencies

• International organizations

• Healthcare systems

• Non-governmental organizations

• Public policy institutions

• Research organizations

The fellowship prepares graduates to take leadership roles in addressing global health challenges and improving health systems worldwide.

Application Process

To be considered for the Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026, applicants must first apply to Cornell’s Master of Public Health program through the SOPHAS application platform.

Applicants must also submit the Cornell Graduate School Quick Application.

Candidates will be evaluated based on their academic achievements, commitment to public health, and potential to contribute to meaningful change in global health systems.

Application Timeline

Applicants must follow the official application timeline to be considered for the fellowship.

Key dates include:

• March 23, 2026: Deadline to apply to Cornell’s MPH program via SOPHAS

• March 23, 2026: Deadline to submit the Cornell Graduate School Quick Application

• March 31, 2026: Applicants notified of fellowship decisions

• April 15, 2026: Deadline to accept the fellowship offer

Meeting these deadlines is essential to ensure eligibility for the fellowship.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Prestigious Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026, click here.

APPLY HERE

Conclusion

The Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for students who aspire to become leaders in public health. By providing full tuition support and access to world-class academic training, the fellowship empowers students to address the complex health challenges facing communities around the world.

Through interdisciplinary learning, practical experience, and specialized training, fellows will develop the skills needed to transform public health systems and improve health outcomes for future generations.

Discover More Fellowship Opportunities

For more fellowship opportunities on OFY Opportunities, click here.

The post Transformative Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 for Future Global Health Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Prestigious Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026 for African Researchers
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Prestigious Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for African scholars to conduct policy-relevant research in Germany while collaborating with leading research institutions and contributing to global discussions on Africa’s future. This fellowship program is designed to support highly qualified researchers from African countries who are engaged in analyzing the structural transformations shaping […]

The post Prestigious Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026 for African Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Prestigious Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for African scholars to conduct policy-relevant research in Germany while collaborating with leading research institutions and contributing to global discussions on Africa’s future. This fellowship program is designed to support highly qualified researchers from African countries who are engaged in analyzing the structural transformations shaping the continent.

The fellowship is part of the Megatrends Afrika project, an initiative that conducts interdisciplinary research on major trends affecting Africa and provides policy advice to decision-makers. The project is a collaboration between German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP), German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS), and the Kiel Institute for the World Economy.

Through this prestigious fellowship, selected scholars will spend three months in Germany during fall 2026 conducting independent research and engaging with policy experts and academics. Fellows will also contribute a research paper to the project’s publication series.

The application deadline for the Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026 is March 25, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. CET.

Fellowship Overview and Research Opportunities

The Megatrends Fellowship is designed to support African researchers who are conducting policy-relevant academic work on Africa’s evolving political, economic, and social dynamics.

Selected fellows will spend three months working full-time on their research project at one of the partner institutions in Germany. Fellows will either conduct their research independently or collaborate with members of the Megatrends Afrika project team or other researchers affiliated with the host institutions.

During the fellowship, participants will also be encouraged to engage with ongoing research activities, seminars, and events organized by the host institutes. These engagements provide an opportunity for fellows to exchange ideas with international scholars and policymakers working on Africa-related issues.

A key outcome of the fellowship is the production of a Megatrends Policy Brief, which will be published as part of the project’s research outputs.

Research Themes in the Megatrends Fellowship

The fellowship invites research proposals addressing important structural transformations shaping Africa’s future.

Applicants are encouraged to submit research proposals aligned with one of the following core themes:

Peace and Security

Research proposals under this theme may explore issues related to conflict prevention, regional security cooperation, peacebuilding strategies, and evolving security dynamics across the African continent.

Future of Democracy

This theme focuses on governance, democratic institutions, political reforms, and citizen participation in African countries.

Researchers may examine political transitions, democratic resilience, and governance challenges affecting African states.

Economic Prospects

The fellowship supports research examining Africa’s economic transformation and development prospects.

Topics may include economic diversification, regional trade integration, sustainable development strategies, and economic policy reforms.

Africa’s Role in the Global Order

This theme explores Africa’s growing influence in global governance and international relations.

Researchers may investigate Africa’s partnerships with global powers, its role in multilateral institutions, and its evolving geopolitical significance.

Science Diplomacy and International Cooperation

This research area focuses on the role of scientific collaboration and international partnerships in addressing global challenges and advancing development in Africa.

Emerging Topics of Interest

In addition to the core research themes, the program is also interested in proposals exploring emerging trends affecting Africa.

These include topics such as:

• Artificial intelligence and new technologies in African countries

• Africa’s energy transition and international cooperation

• Critical raw materials and global supply chains

• Digitalization and its influence on governance

• Urbanization and its impact on political systems

These emerging topics reflect the rapidly changing global landscape and its implications for Africa’s future development.

Fellowship Benefits and Financial Support

The Megatrends Afrika Fellowship provides financial and logistical support to ensure that fellows can focus on their research during their stay in Germany.

Selected fellows will receive:

• A monthly grant of €2,800

• An economy-class return flight between their home country and Germany

• Additional financial support for public transport, mobile phone credit, and other living expenses

• Assistance with visa applications, including an official support letter if required

These benefits help ensure that fellows can fully dedicate their time to research and academic collaboration.

Fellowship Location and Duration

The fellowship will take place in fall 2026 and will last for three months.

Selected fellows will be hosted at one of the partner institutions:

• Berlin – German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP)

• Bonn – German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS)

• Kiel – Kiel Institute for the World Economy

During their stay, fellows will reside in the city of their host institution and actively participate in academic and research activities.

Application Process

Applicants must submit their fellowship application by email as a single PDF document.

The application package must include:

• A one-page motivation letter

• An academic CV including a list of publications

• Two recent academic publications

• Copies of relevant academic transcripts or diplomas

• A project concept note (maximum 2,000 words)

The concept note should clearly outline the research topic, its academic and policy relevance, methodological approach, and feasibility within the three-month fellowship period.

Applications must be sent to the following email address:

Megatrends-Afrika@swp-berlin.org

Selection Process

Applications will be evaluated by a selection committee consisting of researchers from the participating institutions.

Selection decisions will be based on several key criteria, including:

• Academic merit and research track record

• Relevance of the research project to the Megatrends Afrika themes

• Feasibility of completing the research within the fellowship period

• Ability to communicate research findings clearly to both academic and policy audiences

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to participate in an online interview.

Deadline for Applications

The deadline to apply for the Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026 is:

📅 March 25, 2026 (11:59 p.m. CET)

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Prestigious Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026 for African Researchers, click here

Please send your application by email to Megatrends-Afrika@swp-berlin.org.

Conclusion
